description: US-10 Archive a Customer — Manual archiving with a recorded reason frees the customer number for reassignment while keeping the full record. Consecutive no-shows are made visible so that archiving trigger is usable.

userStories.title: consecutiveNoShows counting rule
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to see how many of my own distribution days a customer has missed in a row, so the archiving trigger is actually usable. Source: tasks/prd-us-10-archive-customer.md §US-10.1
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/distribution/noShows.ts exports consecutiveNoShows({records, customerGroup, registeredOn, settings, today})
Only weeks whose colour matches the customer's group are candidates — the other group's weeks are not misses
Counting stops at the most recent attendance record
No misses are counted for weeks before the customer's registration date
Weeks during which the customer was blocked are counted, and the decision is documented in a comment
Tests cover: never attended, attended last time, missed one, missed three, a group change mid-history, and a customer registered two weeks ago
today and settings are parameters — no clock in the module
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: archiveCustomer use case
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to archive a customer with a reason, so their slot frees up and the record is kept. Source: tasks/prd-us-10-archive-customer.md §US-10.2
acceptanceCriteria: archiveCustomer(deps, {customerId, reason}) transitions the status to ARCHIVED via the status state machine and stores the reason
An empty or whitespace-only reason rejects with MissingAuditReason("customer.archived") — the existing error in errors.ts, not a new class
Test asserts the freed number is returned by lowestFreeNumber on the next call
Test counts rows before and after to prove distribution records, cards, certificates, reminder logs and notes are all retained unchanged
The customerNumber is retained on the archived row for historical reference
Archiving an already-archived customer rejects; archiving a blocked customer is allowed
Writes an audit entry carrying the reason
Tested against hand-written fakes and a fake clock
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Archive fields and no-delete invariants
userStories.description: As a developer, I need archiving to be a status change that frees the slot while keeping everything queryable. Source: tasks/prd-us-10-archive-customer.md §US-10.3
acceptanceCriteria: Customer.archiveReason String? and Customer.archivedAt DateTime? added
Repository test proves an archived row does not appear in takenActiveNumbers()
Repository test proves two archived customers may hold the same customerNumber
No cascading deletes exist anywhere in schema.prisma
Migration committed under prisma/migrations/
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Archive UI and no-show visibility
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to archive a customer with a reason and see consecutive no-shows, so I can spot who is no longer attending. Source: tasks/prd-us-10-archive-customer.md §US-10.4
acceptanceCriteria: 'Archivieren' action on the customer detail page with a required reason field; save is disabled until the reason is non-empty
A confirmation step states in German that the customer number will be freed and may be reassigned, and that the record is kept
The customer detail page and the counter screen display the consecutive-no-show count when it is greater than zero
The counter screen shows the reminder count beside an expired certificate and keeps archiving reachable as an ordinary action — no prompt, no blocking modal
An archived customer's record renders read-only with an archived banner showing the reason and date
All user-facing strings come from src/i18n/de.ts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: E2E: archiving frees the customer number
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Playwright spec proving the slot-reuse mechanic end to end. Source: tasks/prd-us-10-archive-customer.md §US-10.5
acceptanceCriteria: Spec registers a customer, notes their number, archives them with a reason, registers a new customer and asserts the freed number is reassigned
Spec asserts the archived customer is still findable and their distribution records are intact
Spec asserts the archived customer cannot be served at the counter
Spec asserts archiving without a reason is refused
npm run test:e2e passes
Typecheck passes
